description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that defines three hidden jobs: .define-build-rules, .define-cores, and .intel-install. The .define-build-rules job sets rules so it runs automatically on merge request events and requires a manual trigger for all other pipeline sources. The .define-cores job runs a script that extracts the number of CPU cores and sockets via lscpu, computes N_PROC and M, chooses an appropriate mpirun command for NEW_DALTON_LAUNCHER, writes N_PROC, M and DALTON_LAUNCHER to var_cores.env, sources the file, exports DALTON_LAUNCHER, and echoes the core count and launcher settings. The .intel-install job uses a before_script to update the system with dnf, install git, python, wget, cmake and other tools, add the Intel oneAPI repository, install Intel compilers, MKL, MPI packages, set up module files, purge existing modules, list available modules, and finally load tbb, compiler-rt, compiler, mkl and mpi modules.
